title: Matrix Builds and Job Dependencies
description:
## Fan Out, Then Gate

**Your task:** write a GitHub Actions workflow at **`/root/matrix.yml`** that:
1. has a **`test`** job using a **`strategy.matrix`** over **`node: [18, 20]`**
2. has a **`deploy`** job that runs **only after** `test` succeeds (via **`needs: test`**)

> 💡 A matrix runs the job once per combination; `needs` creates an ordering/dependency edge. Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/matrix.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Matrix + needs
hints.body: Put strategy.matrix under the test job; give deploy a needs key pointing at test so it waits.
hints.command:
printf 'on: push
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [18, 20]
    steps:
      - run: echo testing ${{ matrix.node }}
  deploy:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo deploying
' > /root/matrix.yml

validation.description: Checks the test job has a matrix and the deploy job needs test.

commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/matrix.yml')); t=d['jobs']['test']; print('ok' if 'matrix' in t.get('strategy',{}) else 'no')"
python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/matrix.yml')); n=d['jobs']['deploy'].get('needs'); print('ok' if n=='test' or (isinstance(n,list) and 'test' in n) else 'no')"
